title: Target Quadruplets
description:
Return all unique quadruplets [a,b,c,d] such that they sum to target. The result should not contain duplicates. Sorting plus two pointers in the inner loop achieves an efficient solution.

    

Constraints:
    - 4 ≤ len(nums) ≤ 200
- -10^4 ≤ nums[i], target ≤ 10^4
explanation:
Intuition
After sorting, choose two indices for the first half and run a classic two pointer scan for the remaining pair. Skip duplicates at all levels to keep the output unique.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Sort the array.
2. Main Algorithm: Loop a and b. For each pair, sweep l and r inward and collect matches while skipping equal values.
3. Return Result: Return the list of unique quadruplets.

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n^3) because the scan adjusts pointers a limited
  number of times and avoids nested loops.
- Space Complexity: O(1) since only a few variables or an output
  list are used.

Key Insights
- Dedup at a, b, l, and r is essential
- Two pointer inner loop avoids a third nested loop

Common Pitfalls
- Not skipping duplicates leads to repeated results
- Using sets can complicate ordering and cost extra space
hints: Sort first to enable skipping duplicates
Fix two indices and scan the rest with two pointers
Move pointers past equal values after a hit
